name: rad-okf
description:
Build and maintain an Open Knowledge Format (OKF) knowledge base from Claude Code — for developers consolidating a repo's knowledge and for non-technical knowledge workers building an AI-readable second brain. OKF bundles are plain directories of markdown files with YAML frontmatter: human-readable, git-friendly, and consumable by any AI agent without an SDK or server.

The plugin keeps a bundle healthy and hard to let rot, with a dependency-free stdlib Python 3 engine that never overwrites your files without a preview.

Authoring and upkeep: `start` (guided onboarding), `new` (scaffold a concept or bundle), `add` (import existing markdown), `move` (link-safe rename/relocate), `check` (validate conformance — frontmatter + required `type`, broken cross-links, orphaned and stale concepts, index drift — with a preview-gated `--fix`), and `map` (self-contained, zero-dependency HTML graph), plus an always-on `okf` conventions skill. Ingestion: `convert` (non-markdown → concept), `scan` (triage a repo or folder for importable knowledge), and `find` (ranked search over the bundle). Sync-agnostic — manage the knowledge here, sync the folder however you like (git, cloud folder, Obsidian, Syncthing).
